H3700.strongs_def: properly, to become pale, i.e. (by implication) to pine after; also to fear

H3701.strongs_def: silver (from its pale color); by implication, money

H3702.strongs_def: {silver (from its pale color); by implication, money}

H3704.strongs_def: a cushion or pillow (as covering a seat or bed)

H3706.strongs_def: thus (only in the formula 'and so forth')

H3707.strongs_def: to trouble; by implication, to grieve, rage, be indignant
H3707.kjv_def: be angry, be grieved, take indignation, provoke (to anger, unto wrath), have sorrow, vex, be wroth.
H3707.usage: anger(43x), provoked(3x), angry(2x), grieved(1x), indignation(1x), sorrow(1x), vex(1x), wrath(1x), wroth(1x)

H3708.kjv_def: anger, angry, grief, indignation, provocation, provoking, [idiom] sore, sorrow, spite, wrath.
H3708.usage: grief(7x), provocation(4x), wrath(4x), sorrow(3x), anger(2x), angry(1x), indignation(1x), provoking(1x), sore(1x), spite(1x)

H3709.strongs_def: the hollow hand or palm (so of the paw of an animal, of the sole, and even of the bowl of a dish or sling, the handle of a bolt, the leaves of a palm-tree); figuratively, power
H3709.kjv_def: branch, [phrase] foot, hand((-ful), -dle, (-led)), hollow, middle, palm, paw, power, sole, spoon.
H3709.usage: hand(128x), spoon(24x), sole(19x), palm(5x), hollow(3x), handful(2x), apiece(1x), branches(1x), clouds(1x)

H3711.strongs_def: properly, to bend, i.e. (figuratively) to tame or subdue

H3713.strongs_def: properly, a cover, i.e. (by implication) a tankard (or covered goblet); also white frost (as covering the ground)

H3714.derivation: from an unused root meaning to connect;

H3715.strongs_def: a village (as covered in by walls); also a young lion (perhaps as covered with a mane)

H3716.derivation: feminine of H3715 (כְּפִיר); the village (always with the art.);

H3717.strongs_def: to fold together; figuratively, to repeat

H3720.strongs_def: hunger (as making to stoop with emptiness and pain)

H3722.strongs_def: to cover (specifically with bitumen); figuratively, to expiate or condone, to placate or cancel
H3722.kjv_def: appease, make (an atonement, cleanse, disannul, forgive, be merciful, pacify, pardon, purge (away), put off, (make) reconcile(-liation).
H3722.usage: atonement(71x), purge(7x), reconciliation(4x), reconcile(3x), forgive(3x), pacify(2x), merciful(2x), cleansed(1x), disannulled(1x), appease(1x), pardon(1x), pitch(1x)

H3724.strongs_def: properly, a cover, i.e. (literally) a village (as covered in); (specifically) bitumen (as used for coating), and the henna plant (as used for dyeing); figuratively, a redemption-price
H3724.kjv_def: bribe, camphire, pitch, ransom, satisfaction, sum of money, village.
H3724.usage: ransom(8x), satisfaction(2x), bribe(2x), camphire(2x), pitch(1x), village(1x)

H3726.derivation: from H3723 (כָּפָר) and H5984 (עַמּוֹנִי), with the article interposed; village of the Ammonite;

H3727.strongs_def: a lid (used only of the cover of the sacred Ark)

H3728.strongs_def: to tread down; figuratively, to humiliate

H3730.derivation: or (Amos 9:1) כַּפְתּוֹר; probably from an unused root meaning to encircle;
H3730.strongs_def: a chaplet; but used only in an architectonic sense, i.e. the capital of acolumn, or a wreath-like button or disk on the candelabrum

H3731.derivation: or (Amos 9:7) כַּפְתּוֹר; apparently the same as H3730 (כַּפְתֹּר);
H3731.strongs_def: Caphtor (i.e. a wreath-shaped island), the original seat of the Philistines

H3732.strongs_def: a Caphtorite (collectively) or native of Caphtor

H3733.derivation: from H3769 (כָּרַר) in the sense of plumpness;
H3733.strongs_def: a ram (as full-grown and fat), including a battering-ram (as butting); hence, a meadow (as for sheep); also a pad or camel's saddle (as puffed out)
H3733.kjv_def: captain, furniture, lamb, (large) pasture, ram. See also H1033 (בֵּית כַּר), H3746 (כָּרִי).

H3734.strongs_def: properly, a deep round vessel, i.e. (specifically) a cor or measure for things dry

H3735.derivation: (Aramaic) probably corresponding to H3738 (כָּרָה) in the sense of piercing(figuratively);

H3737.derivation: (Aramaic) from a verb corresponding to that of H3736 (כַּרְבֵּל);

H3738.strongs_def: properly, to dig; figuratively, to plot; generally, to bore or open

H3739.derivation: usually assigned as a primitive root, but probably only a special application of H3738 (כָּרָה) (through the common idea of planning implied in a bargain);

H3746.derivation: perhaps an abridged plural of H3733 (כַּר) in the sense of leader (of the flock);

H3748.strongs_def: a cutting (of the matrimonial bond), i.e. divorce

H3749.derivation: expanded from the same as H3522 (כַּבּוֹן);

H3753.strongs_def: a dromedary (from its rapid motion as if dancing)

H3754.derivation: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;
H3754.kjv_def: vines, (increase of the) vineyard(-s), vintage. See also H1021 (בֵּית הַכֶּרֶם).

H3755.derivation: active participle of an imaginary denominative from H3754 (כֶּרֶם);
H3755.kjv_def: vine dresser (as one or two words).

H3756.strongs_def: Karmi, the name of three Israelites

H3757.strongs_def: a Karmite or descendant of Karmi

H3759.strongs_def: a planted field (garden, orchard, vineyard or park); by implication, garden produce
H3759.kjv_def: full (green) ears (of corn), fruitful field (place), plentiful (field).

H3760.strongs_def: Karmel, the name of a hill and of a town in Palestine

H3761.strongs_def: a Karmelite or inhabitant of Karmel (the town)

H3762.strongs_def: a Karmelitess or female inhabitant of Karmel

H3766.strongs_def: to bend the knee; by implication, to sink, to prostrate
H3766.kjv_def: bow (down, self), bring down (low), cast down, couch, fall, feeble, kneeling, sink, smite (stoop) down, subdue, [idiom] very.
H3766.usage: bow(14x), fell(2x), subdued(2x), couched(1x), feeble(1x), kneeling(1x), very(1x)

H3767.strongs_def: the leg (from the knee to the ankle) of men or locusts (only in the dual)

H3770.strongs_def: the paunch or belly (as swelling out)

H3772.strongs_def: to cut (off, down or asunder); by implication, to destroy or consume; specifically, to covenant (i.e. make an alliance or bargain, originally by cutting flesh and passing between the pieces)
H3772.kjv_def: be chewed, be con-(feder-) ate, covenant, cut (down, off), destroy, fail, feller, be freed, hew (down), make a league (covenant), [idiom] lose, perish, [idiom] utterly, [idiom] want.
H3772.usage: make(85x), cut(9x), fail(6x), destroy(4x), want(3x), covenanted(2x), hew(2x)

H3773.strongs_def: something cut, i.e. a hewn timber

H3774.derivation: probably from H3772 (כָּרַת) in the sense of executioner; (compare H2876 (טַבָּח)) (only collectively in the singular as plural)

H3777.derivation: from an unused root of uncertain meaning;

H3778.derivation: (occasionally with enclitic) כַּשְׂדִּימָה; towards the Kasdites into Chaldea), patronymically from H3777 (כֶּשֶׂד) (only in the plural);
H3778.strongs_def: a Kasdite, or descendant of Kesed; by implication, a Chaldaean (as if so descended); also an astrologer (as if proverbial of that people

H3779.strongs_def: a Chaldaean or inhabitant of Chaldaea; by implication, a Magian or professional astrologer

H3780.strongs_def: to grow fat (i.e. be covered with flesh)

H3781.strongs_def: properly, a feller, i.e. an axe

H3782.strongs_def: to totter or waver (through weakness of the legs, especially the ankle); by implication, to falter, stumble, faint or fall
H3782.kjv_def: bereave (from the margin), cast down, be decayed, (cause to) fail, (cause, make to) fall (down, -ing), feeble, be (the) ruin(-ed, of), (be) overthrown, (cause to) stumble, [idiom] utterly, be weak.
H3782.usage: fall(27x), stumble(19x), feeble(4x), overthrown(2x), ruin(2x), decayed(1x), faileth(1x), utterly(1x), weak(1x), variant(2x)

H3784.strongs_def: properly, to whisper aspell, i.e. to inchant or practise magic

H3787.derivation: a primitive root properly, to be straight or right;
H3787.strongs_def: by implication, to be acceptable; also to succeed or prosper

H3789.strongs_def: to grave, by implication, to write (describe, inscribe, prescribe, subscribe)

H3790.strongs_def: {to grave, by implication, to write (describe, inscribe, prescribe, subscribe)}

H3791.strongs_def: something written, i.e. a writing, record or book

H3792.strongs_def: {something written, i.e. a writing, record or book}

H3793.strongs_def: a letter or other mark branded on the skin

H3794.derivation: or כִּתִּיִּי; patrial from an unused name denoting Cyprus (only in the plural);
H3794.strongs_def: a Kittite or Cypriote; hence, an islander in general, i.e. the Greeks or Romans on the shores opposite Palestine

H3796.derivation: from an unused root meaning to compact;

H3798.derivation: from H3796 (כֹּתֶל) and H376 (אִישׁ); wall of a man;

H3799.strongs_def: properly, to carve or engrave, i.e. (by implication) to inscribe indelibly
